title: Heading Versus Radial
disclaimer: Training support only. Confirm phraseology, minima, and local procedures with an instructor and current official references.
learning_objectives: Explain the difference between an assigned heading and an assigned radial.
Use a radial instruction to return an aircraft to a defined navigation line.
Resume normal navigation after a radial deviation.
key_points: Heading is where the aircraft nose is pointed.
A radial is a navigation line referenced from a fix or navaid.
An aircraft assigned a heading may keep drifting from its route until another instruction is issued.
An aircraft assigned a radial should intercept the radial line and then track that line.
Resume normal navigation sends the aircraft back toward its planned route, not the last temporary radial.

lesson_steps.title: Heading
lesson_steps.body: A heading instruction tells the aircraft which direction to fly. It is useful for tactical vectoring, but it can take the aircraft away from its planned route if left unchanged.

lesson_steps.title: Radial
lesson_steps.body: A radial instruction references a navigation line. The aircraft turns to intercept the assigned radial and then follows it until another instruction changes the lateral mode.

lesson_steps.title: What To Watch On The Map
lesson_steps.body: Watch the heading vector, the route line, the assigned radial state, and the radial cross-track value. Use the route/fix inspection and ruler tools if needed.

lesson_steps.title: Demonstration
script: Select an en-route or overflight aircraft.
Watch the aircraft intercept the radial before tracking it.
Use Resume Nav to return the aircraft to its route.

exercise.title: Intercept A Radial And Resume Route
exercise.objective: Assign a radial to create a controlled route deviation, monitor capture, then resume normal navigation.
student_actions: Inspect its route and nearby fixes.
Assign a radial different from its current route bearing.
Monitor heading, radial state, and cross-track.
Resume normal navigation after the aircraft is established.
success_conditions: Aircraft receives a valid radial instruction.
Aircraft enters radial tracking after intercept.
Aircraft is returned to route using Resume Nav.
debrief_prompts: Was the instruction a heading or radial instruction?
Did the aircraft intercept before tracking the radial?
How far did the aircraft deviate from its route?
